era_context: Late Bronze Age aftermath; Iron Age consolidation. Egypt (Ramesses XI, 20th Dynasty); Zhou Dynasty (Western Zhou). This is a period of state recovery and reorganization across Eurasia following the disruptions of the 12th century BCE.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed (no events listed for this year).
historiographic_note: 1059 BCE is archaeologically and epigraphically undocumented. No specific events are attested for this year. The historical record for the 11th century BCE is sparse overall; most years in this period leave no trace in surviving sources. The few documented events come from Egypt and China, reflecting the bias of the surviving record toward literate, monumental civilizations.
